description_long: This object is a golden-brown, rectangular pastry with a slightly domed top and visible, tightly coiled layers at the ends, characteristic of a pain au chocolat. The surface is smooth with a subtle sheen, indicating a light egg wash, and the edges are slightly crispier and darker than the rest of the body. The sides reveal a delicate, flaky structure, while the top is more uniform in color and texture. The pastry appears to be made from laminated dough, with thin, airy layers visible at the ends. There are no visible toppings or fillings on the exterior, but the shape and structure suggest a soft, airy interior. The overall appearance is appetizing and typical of a fresh bakery item found in urban cafes or bakeries.
description: A golden-brown, rectangular croissant with visible flaky layers at the ends and a smooth, slightly glossy top. The pastry looks airy, crisp on the outside, and soft inside.
description_view_0: The end view shows the tightly coiled, spiral layers of the croissant, highlighting its flaky, laminated structure.
description_view_1: The side view displays the smooth, slightly domed top and the uniform golden-brown color, with minimal visible layering.
description_view_2: The opposite end view again reveals the spiral, flaky layers, similar to view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: The other side view shows the broad, smooth surface of the croissant, with a slightly darker, more baked appearance.
